ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: If you've been using this modem with another internet provider, 'PPPoE' may be selected. Change 'WAN Connection Type' to 'Automatic IP' and clear any username and password fields.
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box to the blue WAN port on the router
Open a web browser and go to http://router.asus.com (or http://192.168.50.1)
Log in with your admin credentials (set during initial setup)
Clear any PPPoE username/password fields if visible
Wait 1-2 minutes for the router to obtain an IP address from the nbn connection box

wan_config.vlan_notes: No VLAN configuration needed for Belong. If the router was previously configured with a VLAN (e.g. VLAN 2 for TPG), go to LAN > IPTV and set Connection Type to 'None'.

physical.wan_port_position: Second Ethernet port from the left when viewed from rear (to the right of the 2.5G port)

factory_reset.method: Press and hold the Reset button on the rear panel for 5-10 seconds until the power LED starts flashing, then release. The router will reboot and launch the Quick Internet Setup (QIS) wizard on next access.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset does NOT restore to admin/admin — it clears all settings and re-launches the initial setup wizard where you create new admin credentials. Alternative: use the web GUI at Administration > Restore/Save/Upload Setting > click Restore. If the hardware reset button doesn't fully clear settings, try the WPS button method: power off the router, hold the WPS button, power on, release when the power LED turns off.

firmware.notes: ASUSWRT supports automatic firmware checking from the admin panel. Merlin firmware (asuswrt-merlin.net) is a popular community alternative but not recommended for non-technical users. Update to latest official firmware before configuring for Belong.

setup.setup_notes: The RT-AX86U is a retail standalone router — no DSL modem, so only wan_config.ethernet applies (FTTP/FTTC/HFC only, not FTTN/B). ASUS's ASUSWRT interface is consistent across models, and the WAN setting is straightforward. Key gotcha: the 2.5G port looks like it should be the WAN port (it's the first port from the left) but it's LAN-only on this model — customers must use the blue port next to it. Also: when switching from another router on HFC, the nbn NTD may need up to 30 minutes powered off to release the previous DHCP lease before the new router can connect.

setup_sources.description: ASUS AU — How to set up Internet Connection (WAN connection types)
Whirlpool — NBN HFC setup for ASUS RT-AC86U (same ASUSWRT UI)
ASUS — How to reset router to factory default setting
ASUS — How to update firmware via WebGUI
SNBForums — RT-AX86U port identification discussion (WAN vs 2.5G)

setup_confidence.notes: Admin IP: 192.168.50.1 confirmed for AX-series by ASUS FAQ and router-reset.com, though some older firmware may use 192.168.1.1 — noted router.asus.com as primary access method to avoid confusion (-5 for IP ambiguity). No default credentials — QIS wizard confirmed by ASUS FAQ. WAN nav path (WAN > Internet Connection > Automatic IP) confirmed by ASUS AU official FAQ with clear field labels. Physical port layout (blue WAN second from left, 2.5G LAN-only first from left) confirmed by SNBForums and ASUS product page. Factory reset (5-10 sec hold) confirmed by ASUS FAQ. HFC DHCP lease gotcha confirmed by Whirlpool. Score: 100 - 5 (IP ambiguity) - 10 (no second source specifically for RT-AX86U nav path — used RT-AC86U Whirlpool thread which has same ASUSWRT UI) = 85.
